pi_items.source: Task description specifies 'Enter 480 in the Line Voltage field' for a three-phase system

Three-phase voltage conversion formula: V_phase = V_line / √3 = 480 / 1.732 = 277.128 V. Verified against multiple electrical engineering sources (https://www.studyforfe.com/blog/how-to-calculate-three-phase-values/, https://theengineeringmindset.com/three-phase-voltage-calculations/)
Valid tolerance specification for verification. Calculated value 277.128 V is well within ±1.0 V tolerance range (276.1-278.1 V)
Path specified in task.json and referenced in task description (line 6): 'Save a screenshot of the result to /sdcard/tasks/three_phase_voltage_conversion_result.png'

privileged_info_summary: This task involves calculating phase voltage from a 480V three-phase line voltage using the standard electrical engineering formula V_phase = V_line / √3. The expected phase voltage is 277.1 volts (calculated value: 277.128 V), which is verified against authoritative electrical engineering sources. The input voltage of 480V and tolerance of 1.0V are both appropriate specifications. All metadata values are mathematically correct and consistent with three-phase Y-connected system standards. The task uses a synthetic calculation scenario with no real-world data sources.
